GET THE DEAL
GET
THE
DEAL
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 11 --

Pitches are transmitted from the modern—and smart—part of the brain: the neocortex.
But they are received by a part of the brain that is 5 million years older (and not as
bright): the croc brain. The Croc brain is picky and a cognitive miser whose
primary instinct is survival. It is going to ignore you if possible- and won’t do a lot of
work to hear about your idea.
Beware the Croc Brain: gatekeeper of the mind The Croc Brain	3
1
2
Learn The
It’s easy for your audience to
get distracted in the middle of
your pitch. Get and keep your
audience’s attention through-
out by provocatively using the
forces of tension, including
risk, danger and
uncertainty—as well as time
constraint.
Every meeting, pitch and
presentation is a social
encounter that is governed by
frames. Frames are a point of
view, a perspective—a
position. Frames don’t combine
or mix. They collide. The
strongest frame always wins.
When people make
decisions, they often
lapse into an analytical
mindset, which can be
your greatest obstacle to
closing a deal. You must
grab your audience’s
attention with a provoca-
tive narrative to create
intrigue and keep their
attention.
You must
understand how
to turn the tables
in any arrange-
ment so your
target is chasing
you to win your
attention and
respect.
Most final decisions are
made on emotion, not
logic. You must lay the
final groundwork to get
your audience fully
emotionally engaged in
your idea.
The last few minutes of a
meeting are the most
critical. Most of us act
“needy” at this point.
You must master the art
of pulling away.
presents
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	GCOMPLETE
Method
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 11 --

THE BUYER wants the deal you are offering. He has agreed verbally to pay
for it, do the deal or to “go ahead.” He may have even signed the docs,
the purchase order or the order form. But ... you still have to get money
transferred from their account to yours.
GET
THE
DEAL
The last few minutes of a meeting are the most critical.
Most of us act “needy” at this point. You must master
the art of pulling away and giving the buyer autonomy:
a sense of self determination.
S	T	R	O	N	
G
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 11 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
Overview: Get the Deal by Providing the Buyer Some Autonomy
Research suggests weeem to be hardwired to desire autonomy. If we feel
coerced by external pressures - like other people telling us to buy their
product — our feeling of autonomy vanishes.
If you are crowding your buyer at this point in the deal, he’s going to lose a sense of autonomy. When you limit, reduce or take away
your buyer’s sense of self-determination, you put the entire deal at risk.
Consider for a moment, why have people throughout
history been willing to fight and even die for their
freedom? Perhaps the answer is obvious: oppression
causes suffering and we’re all hardwired to flee suffering.
But recent research suggests another reason: we also
seem to be hardwired to desire autonomy.
Autonomy can be defined as the ability to make choices
according to one’s own free will. If we feel coerced
by external pressures like other people — our feeling
of autonomy vanishes. Autonomy is the capacity of a
rational individual to make an informed, un-coerced
decision.
It turns out that restrictions on our autonomy may lie
at the heart of a great deal of our unhappiness. Studies
show, for example, that one of the greatest sources of
dissatisfaction among doctors isn’t having to deal with
insurance companies or paperwork but lack of control
over their daily schedules.
If we are told by an authority figure, a person of high
status or even a peer to do something – even if it
something we like — exercise, for example — we tend to
resent it and will actually want to resist doing it in order
to preserve a sense of autonomy.
We always, in fact, have the power to say yes or no.
If that power is threatened in any way, we becomeful
fearful, resentful and stressed.
In deal making, ONLY when the other person we are
dealing with gives us the CHOICE to say yes or no, do we
feel a decision is our own to make, and we experience
the pleasure of autonomy.
GET
THE
DEAL
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 11 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
Unfortunately, money has not transacted yet. It’s one thing to get a verbal
agreeement, or to get a signed purchase order. It can be more difficult to get
the money: that’s why we need to know how to Get the Deal.
GET
THE
DEAL
“There’s many a slip between the cup and the lip.”
“Today I’ve shown you we
have the best / fastest / most
valuable [whatever it is.] But
of course I think that, I work
here and I’m bias.
I can only give you the
information, but can’t tell you
what to do:
You need to decide for
yourself.”
Consider your options:
After your buyer agrees he wants the deal, it’s time to get him to sign the
contract and pay; to transfer funds or make whatever exchange of value you
agreed to. You can do this by moving away and providing him autonomy.
How I will provide my buyer autonomy and leave
him with a sense of self determination in making
the final decision:
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 11 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
IT’S TIME TO CLOSE THE DEAL:
SIGN THE DOCS AND GET THE FUNDS
When we map out the entire pitch process from start to finish, it
can be overwhelming. This is why we have broken Pitch Mastery
into six modules and its important to work the skills module by
module.
As part of “Get the Deal” we are including the graphical
breakdown of the entire process, so you can see how the modules
all work together. Additionally, you can see what happens to your
Status as you work through an hour long meeting.
THE BUYER wants the deal you are offering. He has agreed verbally to pay
for it, do the deal or to “go ahead.” He may have even signed the docs,
the purchase order or the order form. But ... you still have to get money
transferred from their account to yours.
S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
S.T.R.O.N.G MODEL FOR PITCH ANYTHING
STATUS
PRIZING
DEAL

2. PRICE
3. TIMELINE
4. PROCESS

TENSION & NOVELTY
- YOU CAN’T HAVE IT
- APPLY FOR IT
- MORE YOU HAVENT SEEN
REBOOT AUDIENCE BRAIN
SET NEW FRAME
CONTINUE PITCH

QUESTIONS

-- 7 of 11 --

S 	T 	R 	O 	N 	G
S.T.R.O.N.G MODEL FOR PITCH ANYTHING
STATUS
DEAL
G
When we map out the entire pitch process from
start to finish, it can be overwhelming. This is why
we have broken Pitch Mastery into six modules
and its important to work the skills module by
module.
REFLECT AND MAKE NOTES on how to tie-in the STRONG modules to your own
situation. You are now at the point of getting the deal done. If there’s any
resistance to tranferring funds to you, then you’ll need to revisit some of the
strong modules to create deeper wanting.
GET
THE
DEAL
www.getwsodownload.com

IN MANY PITCH SITUATIONS, we see our own status rise and fall. this is map
of how we typically experience the flow of status through the modules. Now
its time to Get the Deal - make sure to maintain your status. Preserve your
thoughts on status in the worksheet below:

the buyer from the point of introducing yourself and your product/idea/
company.
